finding: single-in-flight legacy fallback returns inFlight[0].tool_use_id without verifying that id is present in `contents`, so an unrelated/prior in-flight store call could attach the artifact to a tool_use_id not in this message (stranded orphan / cross-conversation)

relocation is a guaranteed permutation (emitted-guard prevents dup, defensive loop prevents drop); results-before-use, multi-result, orphan, and identity fast-path all correct — no drop/dup/mis-order reproducible

once hasArtifact latches autoOpen true it never re-fires the [autoOpen] effect, so after a user collapses an artifact group a later-started sequential tool will not re-open it; acceptable — respects the deliberate user collapse
while pending_approval force-opens, clicking the chevron toggles userOpen invisibly so post-approval open/closed state depends on click parity; confusing but never stuck (a further click resolves it)
reads b.content unconditionally; a block with null/undefined content would throw a TypeError instead of being skipped (defensive — streaming always sets content, and the type is non-null)

artifactCreated edit is behavior-identical for the common case (event carries tool_use_id → resolveArtifactToolUseId returns it first); only the legacy no-id multi-tool path changed (skips instead of guessing last)

autoOpen latch effect re-opens on every autoOpen false→true edge, so in a sequential run where hasRunning oscillates it re-opens after a user collapse; aligns with the task's 'keep open while running' intent — accepted as designed
reactive read `const { toolCalls } = Stores.McpComposer` matches McpToolUseRenderer; useState/useEffect unconditional and stably ordered; setUserOpen(true) on already-true bails — no infinite loop
Stores.McpComposer.$.toolCalls is the correct hook-free getState() snapshot; SSE handlers and this read are serialized on the single JS event loop — no data race
legacy no-id fallback's single-in-flight heuristic depends on store-commit ordering; only affects backends omitting tool_use_id, where skipping is the safe outcome

McpToolGroupCard subscribes to the whole toolCalls Map (O(mapSize) shallow-compare + re-render on any status change); matches the pre-existing per-tool McpToolUseRenderer subscription pattern and reactivity is required for auto-open-on-approval — accepted
normalizeToolResultOrder is O(n) per render, returns the input reference on the no-tool_result fast path (safe — bubbleBlocks is read-only), and does not break ChatMessage's memo

the legacy no-id fallback's second branch keys off the GLOBAL toolCalls map (not scoped to this conversation), so a single in-flight call elsewhere could mis-attribute; fix by scoping candidates to this message's tool_use ids

resolveArtifactToolUseId strengthens the misattribution guard (null instead of guessing last), touches only grouping/display, does not alter ingest/SSRF/trust policy or expose host paths — link.uri stays authenticated /api/files/{id}
change only affects client-side ordering + default-open; file bytes still resolve via the authenticated /api/files/{id} path; no permission gate or authorization surface modified
status comparisons use 'pending_approval' and 'started', exactly matching the McpToolCall.status union; the 'running' literal at :310 is separate ToolStatusIcon display vocabulary, not a mismatch
field access matches types.ts: resource_links (optional array), tool_use_id (required string), tool_use.id (required), McpToolCall.tool_use_id/.status — all correct shapes
fast identity exit preserves array identity for user/plain-text messages, orphan tool_results stay in place — non-tool and user-message rendering is byte-for-byte unchanged

group default-open on running/artifact + pending_approval force-open; useEffect deps ([autoOpen]) can't loop; MessageFilesView per-block rendering + contentSpan/collectToolRun contiguous folding untouched — no correctness regression
for a lone-tool file-only tool_result appended after trailing text, normalization hoists the file preview ahead of that text — intended (result carries only files) and drops/duplicates nothing

new helpers follow the util+colocated-node:test idiom and have no layering violation; collectToolRun/countToolUses remain inline+untested in extension.tsx (pre-existing, unchanged) — partial extraction, acceptable
reactive store-read in McpToolGroupCard is idiomatic (matches McpToolUseRenderer) and the non-reactive $.toolCalls is correctly used in the event handler, not in render

'collapsed by default' assertion relies on the post-stream reload remounting the group so the transient 'started' auto-open latch resets; if the reload does not fire or the persisted id equals the streaming id, the latch could leave it open and flake — make it deterministic (persisted-only, no streaming tool events)

unit tests are meaningful and cover the real edge cases; the e2e specs assert the right things (artifact INSIDE mcp-toolgroup-card AND visible without a click distinguishes wrap vs auto-open); no false-green found
force-open (pending approval) makes the collapse Button a visual no-op with tooltip 'Hide details'; control stays focusable with an accessible name — minor, the approval visibility is the priority
the group toggle lacks aria-expanded so open/closed state is not announced, but this is consistent with the pre-existing McpToolCallUI/McpToolUseRenderer toggles and not introduced by this change
'{n} tools called' and 'Show/Hide details' are hardcoded English but the repo ships no i18n framework (matches every other string); group only renders for ≥2 tools so the plural is never wrong

names are clear and consistent (runToolUseIds/hasArtifactInRun/shouldAutoOpen/deriveGroupOpen/resolveArtifactToolUseId; toolRun.ts alongside collectToolRun/countToolUses; normalizeToolResultOrder self-describing)
